input: check my calendar and tell me what all events do I have in March
context: Today is mid-March (March 15). Several past events exist in March.
expected_behavior: Only lists events from today (March 15) onward. Does not list past March events. Clearly notes it is showing remaining events only.

input: what do I have this week?
context: It is Wednesday. Some events are Monday and Tuesday.
expected_behavior: Shows only Wednesday through Sunday events. Does not include Monday or Tuesday.

input: do I have any meetings next week?
context: It is currently Friday.
expected_behavior: Shows next week only (Monday through Sunday). Correct date range. Lists count and times.

input: tldr my calendar for the week
context: It is Monday. 6 events spread across the week.
expected_behavior: Extremely concise. Tight bullet list or 1-2 lines. Respects the 'tldr' signal — does not dump full event details.

input: you should only tell me about events from today and in the future
context: Bot previously showed past events in its calendar response.
expected_behavior: Acknowledges the correction without defensiveness. Confirms the new rule. Does not re-list old events.

input: stop showing me events that already happened
context: Nischal is frustrated — bot showed past events again.
expected_behavior: Apologizes briefly, confirms understanding, does not repeat mistake. Does not over-explain.

context: Nischal sent a calendar request 30 seconds ago, bot has not replied yet.
expected_behavior: Interprets as a follow-up nudge. Acknowledges and indicates it is working or asks what he needs. Short response. Does not treat as a new unrelated request.

context: No prior message in this session.
expected_behavior: Asks a brief clarifying question. Does not hallucinate prior context. Under 20 words.

context: Bot was doing a long calendar operation.
expected_behavior: Confirms it is still running. Brief. Does not apologize excessively.

context: Bot just gave a response Nischal acknowledged.
expected_behavior: Waits for next instruction or asks if there is anything else. Does not generate unsolicited follow-up content.

input: How will you make sure you remember this preference?
context: Nischal just stated a preference about future-only calendar events.
expected_behavior: Honest about no cross-session memory. Suggests a practical workaround (CLAUDE.md or pinned note). Does not falsely promise memory.

input: Will you remember what we talked about next time I message you?
context: General question about memory.
expected_behavior: Clear honest no. Brief explanation of session scope. Practical suggestion. Under 50 words.

input: can you add this to your memory?
context: Nischal wants to persist a preference.
expected_behavior: Explains the memory situation clearly. Offers to write to CLAUDE.md or suggests a practical mechanism. Does not silently ignore the request or falsely claim it saved.

context: Nischal just asked bot to apply a behavior change (future-only calendar).
expected_behavior: Acknowledges test mode. Prompts Nischal with what to test or performs a test action if context is clear. Does not stall or just say 'sure'.

context: After configuring the future-only calendar filter.
expected_behavior: Performs a real verification action (checks calendar) or asks what specifically to verify. Does not just say 'yes it works'.

context: 5 calendar events today.
expected_behavior: Compact summary, not a verbatim calendar dump. Mobile-friendly: short lines, bullets. No excessive headers. Under 80 words.

input: what should I focus on this afternoon?
context: It is 1pm. 3 events remain today.
expected_behavior: One clear prioritized recommendation with brief reasoning. Not just a list of all events. Under 60 words.

input: give me a quick rundown of this week
context: It is Monday morning. 8 events across the week.
expected_behavior: Tight summary. Groups by day or theme. Does not list all 8 events in full detail. Respects 'quick' signal.

input: check if I have anything tomorrow morning
context: Bot needs to call calendar tool.
expected_behavior: Either shows brief working indicator before result OR flows naturally from action to result. Does not silently pause then dump results without any indication of what it did.

input: what's the latest AI news today?
context: No prior context. Bot needs to use web search.
expected_behavior: Actually performs search (does not confabulate from training data). Reports 3-5 bullet points with source names or dates. Does not say 'I don't have real-time access'.
